Name: Cardiology
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of heart and vascular disorders. Treats conditions like heart failure and arrhythmias.

Name: Dermatology
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of skin, hair, and nail disorders, including skin cancer and eczema.

Name: Dentistry
Description: Comprehensive care for teeth, gums, and oral health, including preventative and restorative treatments.

Name: Ophthalmology
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of eye disorders and vision care, including surgery and corrective lenses.

Name: Pediatrics
Description: Medical care for infants, children, and adolescents, focusing on physical and emotional development.

Name: Obstetrics and Gynecology
Description: Care for women's reproductive health, pregnancy, childbirth, and postnatal care.

Name: Orthopedics
Description: Treatment of musculoskeletal system conditions, including bones, joints, and ligaments.

Name: Neurology
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of disorders affecting the brain, spinal cord, and nervous system.

Name: Psychiatry
Description: Diagnosis, prevention, and treatment of mental, emotional, and behavioral disorders.

Name: Internal Medicine
Description: Prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of adult diseases and chronic conditions.

Name: General Surgery
Description: Surgical treatment for various conditions affecting the abdomen, digestive tract, and endocrine system.

Name: ENT
Description: Care for disorders of the ear, nose, throat, and related structures of the head and neck.

Name: Urology
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of conditions affecting the urinary tract and male reproductive system.

Name: Endocrinology
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of hormone-related conditions like diabetes and thyroid disorders.

Name: Gastroenterology
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of digestive system disorders affecting the stomach and intestines.

Name: Allergy & Immunology
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of immune system disorders, allergies, and asthma.

Name: Anesthesiology
Description: Pain relief and anesthesia administration during surgery and other procedures.

Name: Oncology
Description: Prevention, diagnosis, and treatment of various types of cancer.

Name: Radiology
Description: Use of medical imaging (X-rays, CT, MRI) to diagnose and treat diseases.

Name: Pathology
Description: Examination of tissues and body fluids to diagnose diseases.

Name: Pulmonology
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of respiratory system and lung disorders.

Name: Hematology
Description: Study, diagnosis, and treatment of blood-related disorders and cancers.

Name: Infectious Disease
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of infections caused by bacteria, viruses, and parasites.

Name: Geriatrics
Description: Medical care focusing on the health and needs of elderly patients.

Name: Emergency Medicine
Description: Immediate diagnosis and treatment of acute illnesses and urgent injuries.

Name: Rheumatology
Description: Treatment of arthritis and autoimmune diseases affecting joints and tissues.

Name: Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation
Description: Restoring physical function and quality of life for patients with disabilities.

Name: Plastic Surgery
Description: Surgical procedures for reconstruction or enhancement of physical appearance.

Name: Neurosurgery
Description: Surgical treatment of disorders affecting the brain and spinal cord.

Name: Vascular Surgery
Description: Surgical treatment of disorders affecting blood vessels and circulation.

Name: Nuclear Medicine
Description: Use of radioactive substances for diagnosis and treatment of diseases.

Name: Nephrology
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of kidney-related diseases and conditions.

Name: Maxillofacial Surgery
Description: Surgical treatment of injuries and defects in the head, neck, face, and jaws.

Name: Pediatric Surgery
Description: Surgical care specifically for infants, children, and adolescents.

Name: Family Medicine
Description: Continuous primary healthcare for individuals and families of all ages.

Name: Sports Medicine
Description: Treatment and prevention of injuries related to sports and exercise.

Name: Forensic Medicine
Description: Application of medical knowledge to legal questions and death investigations.

Name: Occupational Medicine
Description: Focuses on the health and safety of people at work.

Name: Palliative Care
Description: Focuses on providing relief from the symptoms and stress of serious illness.

Name: Sleep Medicine
Description: Diagnosis and treatment of sleep disturbances and disorders.
